name: Black Bean and Corn Salsa
recipeIngredient: 2-15 oz. cans black beans, rinsed and drained
1 cup frozen yellow corn kernals, defrosted and drained
1 cup frozen white shoepeg corn kernals, defrosted and drained
1 large sweet onion (Bermuda, Florida, Maui, Vidalia), chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the rinsed and drained black beans with the defrosted yellow corn kernals.
2. Add the chopped cilantro, minced jalapeño, peeled tomato, minced garlic, chopped onion, sliced green onions, chili powder, ground cumin, lime juice, black pepper, and a dash of cayenne pepper.
3. Mix everything together gently until well combined. Adjust quantities to suit your taste preferences.
4. Transfer the salsa to a bowl or container and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, up to 2 days, to allow the flavors to meld together.
5. Before serving, let the salsa come to room temperature.
6. Serve with baked tortilla chips or pita chips.
Recipe notes:
I've taken this salsa to numerous potlucks and people really enjoy it. I've also eaten it for many lunches over time.
